lede: International solidarity coordinator, rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and activist engagement — between France, Africa and Eastern Europe.
about_summary: After several years between NGOs in France and international missions, I have built a career rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and activist engagement — voluntary sector on exile issues in France, positions of responsibility in Central Africa, North Africa, Madagascar and Ukraine. Long committed to gender issues, I draw on my queer journey to inform my professional practice.
current_quote: Emergency preparedness, institutional partnerships, inter-NGO coordination.
about: After several years working with NGOs in France and on international missions, particularly on programmes related to exile, I have built a career rooted in the issues of migration, social justice and activist engagement.
In France, I was strongly involved in the voluntary sector on issues of exile (outreach, reception, support, advocacy), and I made migration policy the main focus of my university research.

Internationally, I have held positions of responsibility in Central Africa, North Africa, Madagascar and Ukraine, on programmes combining health, exile, humanitarian logistics and project coordination. I have also led missions on exploration, project development, awareness-raising and advocacy.

Long committed to gender issues, I draw on my personal experience and queer journey to inform my professional practice. I now wish to strengthen this link between gender and exile in my commitments, and to continue my development towards positions combining coordination, political reflection and concrete action.
